title: Portable Workbench "Woodshop in a Box"
summary: A 1x6 storage box with a plywood-panel bottom and bolt-pivoting 2x3 legs — the legs rotate flat against the underside so the whole bench folds down for transport or storage.
description:
This bench doubles as its own carrying case: a shallow 1x6 box with a plywood project-panel bottom holds tools and hardware, and four 2x3 legs bolt to the sides through pivoting joints rather than fixed screws — so they rotate flat against the box for transport and swing back down into a stable stance for use. That pivot is the one part of this build that needs real precision: drill the bolt holes at the exact marked location on both the leg and the side panel, or the leg won't rotate cleanly without catching.

The legs are cut with a 15° angle at the bottom, which is what gives the bench a stable, slightly splayed stance rather than standing dead vertical — and the corners of each leg get nipped off (shown in the build's reference video) so the leg can actually complete its rotation without hanging up on a sharp corner as it swings.

Before cutting anything, measure your own plywood panel — a 24" x 48" project panel is assumed throughout this cut list, but standard sheet-good sizes vary enough between suppliers that this is worth verifying first rather than discovering a mismatch after the box is built.

materials.name: Plywood project panel, 1/2" or 3/4", 24" x 48"
materials.note: Measure your own panel before cutting — standard sizes vary.

materials.name: Washers, matching
materials.note: Three per bolt — one on each end, one between the wood pieces.

cutList.note: Match to the actual length of your plywood panel.
Width of your plywood panel, minus 1-1/2".
One end cut at 15° off square, longest point measurement.
One end cut at 15° off square, longest point measurement.

steps.title: Measure your plywood and build the box
steps.body: Measure the length and width of your actual plywood panel and adjust the cut list if it isn't exactly 24" x 48". Attach the box sides to the ends with three 2" self-tapping screws and glue per joint. Optional handles can be cut into the ends now.

steps.title: Attach the bottom panel
steps.body: Apply glue to the bottom edge of the assembled 1x6 box and attach the plywood panel with 1-1/4" screws, driven slightly below the surface, at least every 12" and on every corner.

steps.title: Cut and prep the legs
steps.body: Cut the four legs with their bottoms at a 15° angle off square. Mark 1-1/4" down and 1-1/4" in from the square-cut end and drill a hole there sized for your bolts. Nip off the corners of each leg near the pivot point so it can rotate without catching.

steps.title: Mount the pivoting legs
steps.body: Drill matching holes in the box sides at the same marked location on both sides. Insert a bolt through a washer, the box side, another washer, the leg, and a final washer, then secure with a locking nut — tight enough to hold the leg's position but still allow it to pivot.

steps.title: Add the center dividers
steps.body: Position the leg pairs centered on the storage compartment, stacked on top of each other as shown in the reference diagram, and mark with a pencil. Attach the center dividers to secure the legs with 2" or longer screws.

images.alt: Portable workbench storage box with folding pivoting legs
